title: Merge Slot
description: Exclusive access slot for serialized conflict resolution in the merge queue.

title: Add tests and CLI or UI reporting for pathway-vs-technology comparison
description: Add tests and user-facing reporting support for running and inspecting the residential water heating pathway-vs-technology comparison.
acceptance_criteria: Single-method and multi-method technology solves behave as expected; incumbent base year is respected; CLI or UI reporting exposes the comparison clearly.
close_reason: Added CLI reporting for the residential water-heating pathway-vs-technology comparison and tests covering multi-method incumbent mix and single-method heat-pump proxy rows.

title: Add comparison artifact between technology solve and pathway method
description: Add an artifact or report shape for comparing a technology-bundle solve against an aggregate pathway method for the same role.
acceptance_criteria: Comparison captures activity, cost, energy inputs, direct emissions, and relevant constraints; results are stored as validation evidence and are not automatically promoted to authored pathway data.
close_reason: Added validation evidence comparing the residential water-heating pathway representation with the technology-bundle incumbent mix and heat-pump proxy.

title: Add incumbent technology mix calibration
description: Add incumbent mix metadata for the residential water heating technology representation.
acceptance_criteria: Incumbent technology mix rows sum to 1.0 in the anchor year; 2025 active role behavior uses the incumbent mix; calibration evidence and confidence are recorded.
close_reason: Replaced the placeholder technology incumbent with calibrated 2025 shares that sum to 1.0 across gas, resistive electric, heat-pump, and solar-boosted methods.

title: Author technology bundle representation
description: Author a technology bundle representation for residential water heating with methods such as gas storage, resistive electric, heat pump, solar/boosted, or other locally appropriate technologies.
acceptance_criteria: Technology methods have inputs, costs, emissions, constraints, and activity units aligned with the pathway representation.
close_reason: Authored residential water-heating technology-bundle representation with gas, resistive electric, heat-pump, and solar-boosted methods across milestone years.

title: Author aggregate pathway representation
description: Author an aggregate pathway representation for residential water heating suitable for fast whole-system runs.
acceptance_criteria: Pathway representation has at least incumbent and transition/decarbonized pathway methods, incumbent metadata, constraints, and emissions metrics.
close_reason: Authored residential water-heating aggregate pathway representation with incumbent, transition, and near-zero pathway methods plus metadata and validation rows.

title: Define residential water heating role boundary and activity driver
description: Define the physical role boundary and activity driver for residential water heating as a simple technology-representation test case.
acceptance_criteria: Boundary separates water heating from other building services; activity driver is explicit; role can be represented by both pathway and technology bundle variants.
close_reason: Defined residential water-heating boundary, activity driver, representation requirements, and follow-on dependency order.

title: Implement technology representation comparison for a simple role
description: Implement a simple technology representation, recommended for deliver_residential_water_heating, so a technology-bundle solve can be compared against an aggregate pathway representation.
acceptance_criteria: One active technology method forces the role through that method; multiple active technology methods optimize the mix; 2025 uses incumbent technology mix; technology solve can be compared to the aggregate pathway on cost, energy inputs, emissions, and activity; comparison result is stored as validation evidence, not silently promoted to an authored pathway.
notes: Beads does not allow an epic to be blocked directly by a task, so the building migration gate is represented by child tasks depending on simple-msm-pathways-1.3.

title: Add solver and visualization tests for aggregate and decomposed cement
description: Add end-to-end tests and visualization-model checks for switching between aggregate and decomposed cement representations.
acceptance_criteria: Both aggregate and decomposed cement configurations solve; role graph shows the correct active representation; comparison output can inspect activity, cost, energy, and emissions differences.

title: Add double-counting validation for integrated vs decomposed heat boundaries
description: Add validation that integrated aggregate cement pathways and decomposed heat/process child roles cannot double count the same heat, fuel, or emissions boundary.
acceptance_criteria: Tests fail if integrated heat inputs remain active with decomposed heat roles; direct combustion and process emissions boundaries are separately traceable.

title: Implement linked parent activity resolution for decomposed pathway roles
description: Implement the activity-resolution mechanics required for decomposed child roles to inherit or derive activity from the parent role.
acceptance_criteria: Decomposed child activity resolves from parent activity; linked activity is validated; solver request carries child activity without duplicating parent demand.

title: Author decomposed child pathway representations
description: Author child pathway representations for the cement decomposition example, including host-specific kiln heat where it materially matters.
acceptance_criteria: Child roles have pathway or residual methods; parent direct methods are inactive when decomposition is selected; child boundaries are documented.

title: Author aggregate cement pathway representation
description: Author or migrate the aggregate pathway representation for cement/clinker/lime/mineral product production.
acceptance_criteria: Aggregate representation has direct pathway methods, incumbent metadata, activity driver, emissions metrics, and integrated heat/fuel boundary notes.

title: Design cement decomposition boundary and child roles
description: Define the physical boundary for decomposing cement/clinker production, including where host-specific heat generation sits and which emissions remain with calcination or process steps.
acceptance_criteria: Boundary design identifies child roles, activity propagation, heat/fuel/emissions ownership, and double-counting guardrails.

title: Implement pathway-based role decomposition example
description: Implement a pathway-based decomposition example for produce_clinker_cement_lime_and_mineral_products, proving aggregate and decomposed pathway representations can be swapped without double counting.
acceptance_criteria: Aggregate cement pathway and decomposed pathway representation are mutually exclusive; host-specific heat appears only inside the decomposed cement role; parent activity propagates to child roles without double counting; aggregate and decomposed solves can be compared.
notes: Beads does not allow an epic to be blocked directly by a task, so the cement migration gate is represented by child tasks depending on simple-msm-pathways-1.5.

title: Validate whole-system coverage and emissions importance bands
description: Validate that the migrated physical role graph covers the whole system at minimum granularity and exposes emissions importance for visualization.
acceptance_criteria: Every minimum-coverage role has at least one active pathway/residual method; temporary unallocated coverage is visible and counted; role metrics resolve; graph visualization can show whole-system coverage.

title: Create carbon capture, transport, storage, and engineered removal placeholders
description: Create minimum viable physical roles and placeholder methods for point-source capture, CO2 transport/storage, and engineered removals.
acceptance_criteria: Carbon capture, transport, storage, and engineered removal roles exist with pathway or residual method coverage; CO2 physical edges are represented; placeholders are visible as low-confidence where needed.

title: Create biological emissions and land-stock residual or pathway roles
description: Create explicit roles and residual/pathway methods for biological emissions and land carbon stock changes that are not product-output roles.
acceptance_criteria: Livestock biogenic emissions, soil/fertiliser/residue emissions, and land stock-change coverage are explicit; each has an activity driver and emissions importance band.

title: Create chemicals and other material transformation residual or pathway roles
description: Create pathway or residual coverage for chemicals and other material transformation activity not covered by steel or cement.
acceptance_criteria: Chemicals and other material roles have explicit pathway/residual methods; process emissions and energy inputs are not hidden in manufacturing sector residuals.

title: Create non-road transport pathway or residual roles
description: Create minimum viable pathway or residual method coverage for rail, air, marine, and other non-road transport activity.
acceptance_criteria: Non-road transport roles are physically named; each has at least residual method coverage or a documented pathway; residual transport is not left as an opaque sector bucket.

title: Create export-gate iron ore pathway or residual roles
description: Create export-gate iron ore supply coverage bounded to Australian extraction, processing, transport, and port delivery.
acceptance_criteria: Iron ore export role exists at export-gate boundary; overseas steelmaking is excluded from domestic role coverage; activity driver and residual/pathway method coverage exist.

title: Create export-gate coal pathway or residual roles
description: Create export-gate coal supply coverage for thermal and metallurgical coal, bounded to Australian extraction, processing, transport, fugitive emissions, and port delivery.
acceptance_criteria: Thermal and metallurgical coal export roles exist at export-gate boundary; overseas combustion is excluded from domestic role coverage; coal mine fugitive methane is visible.

title: Create residual or pathway coverage for domestic fuel supply roles
description: Create minimum viable pathway or residual method coverage for domestic solid fuel, gaseous fuel, liquid fuel, bioenergy, hydrogen, and derived-fuel supply roles.
acceptance_criteria: Domestic fuel supply roles have at least residual method coverage; baseline activity drivers and emissions importance bands exist; upstream fugitive releases are not hidden.

title: Migrate agriculture, land, removals, waste, and water pathways
description: Move agriculture, land carbon, removals, waste, water, and wastewater coverage into physical roles, separating product output from emissions or stock-change accounting where needed.
acceptance_criteria: Coverage is migrated or explicitly residualized; biological emissions and land carbon stock changes are visible physical/accounting roles; engineered and land removals remain explicit.

title: Migrate steel and cement product pathways
description: Move steel and cement pathways into material transformation roles, preserving useful aggregate pathways while preparing for decomposition examples.
acceptance_criteria: Steel and cement product roles have pathway coverage under material transformation; integrated heat/fuel boundaries are explicit; incumbent metadata and role metrics are available.

title: Migrate road mobility and freight pathways
description: Move road passenger mobility and freight pathways into physical transport roles using doing-word names and physical activity drivers.
acceptance_criteria: Road mobility and freight roles have pathway or residual method coverage; incumbent methods resolve; electricity and fuel dependencies remain visible as physical edges where applicable.
close_reason: Migrated passenger and freight road transport roles to physical doing-word role ids with updated metadata, configs, validation fixtures, and tests.

title: Migrate building service pathways into built-environment roles
description: Move residential and commercial building pathways into built-environment roles and decide which aggregate pathways to keep versus decompose later.
acceptance_criteria: Reusable building pathways are migrated under physical built-environment service roles; residual building coverage is explicit and low-fidelity where needed; future end-use decomposition points are identified.
close_reason: Migrated residential/commercial building service and residual roles to physical graph role ids

title: Migrate electricity supply and grid losses pathways
description: Move electricity supply, grid losses, firming, storage, and own-use coverage into the new physical role graph.
acceptance_criteria: Electricity supply has at least one pathway representation; grid losses/own-use are represented without sector-derived topology; incumbent metadata and emissions importance metrics resolve.
close_reason: Migrated electricity supply and grid losses roles to physical graph role ids and updated consumers

title: Inventory current methods and decide keep, move, merge, or retire
description: Review every current method/pathway and classify it against the new physical role graph as keep, move, merge, retire, or temporary residual.
acceptance_criteria: Inventory identifies disposition for every current method; each kept method has a target physical role and representation; each retired method has a rationale; coverage gaps are listed for follow-up migration issues.
close_reason: Added current method migration inventory with dispositions, target physical roles, and coverage gaps

title: Migrate current pathways into physical role graph
description: Move current library pathways that should survive into the new physical role graph, retire or reshape pathways that are artifacts of the old structure, and file explicit coverage for missing pathway or residual method representations.
acceptance_criteria: Every minimum-coverage physical role has at least one pathway or residual method; migrated pathways sit under the nearest physical role, not an accounting sector; obsolete residual sector buckets are retired or marked temporary unallocated; missing coverage is explicit and visible; whole-system visualization can show active coverage with importance bands.

title: Remove obsolete compatibility surfaces and terminology
description: Remove old family/state compatibility surfaces and terminology after the new physical role graph surfaces replace them.
acceptance_criteria: No family/state compatibility layer remains; old role/sector-shaped topology surfaces are removed or replaced; docs and tests use role, representation, method, activity driver, incumbent, and physical graph terminology.
close_reason: Removed generated family/state compatibility aliases from package data and canonicalized consumers

title: Update role graph UI and CLI model to render physical hierarchy and edges
description: Update the role graph model and any CLI-facing structure output so the first view renders physical hierarchy nodes and physical edges, with expandable role representations.
acceptance_criteria: Collapsed role graph starts from physical doing-word nodes; roles expand into representations and methods; decomposition children appear only under active decomposition representations; emissions importance bands are available for styling.
close_reason: Landed physical hierarchy role graph rendering

title: Update loader, validation, and tests for the new library surfaces
description: Update package loading and validation to read the new physical role graph, activity driver, incumbent, and metric surfaces.
acceptance_criteria: Loader exposes the new surfaces; package tests validate node hierarchy, role memberships, physical edges, activity drivers, incumbent mixes, and metrics; old sector-derived topology assumptions are removed.
close_reason: Landed loader exposure for physical graph and metric surfaces

title: Add role emissions importance metrics and bands
description: Add role-level emissions importance metadata for visualization, computed from baseline/default representation data where possible and exposed as coarse bands.
acceptance_criteria: Role metrics surface exists; baseline direct gross emissions and importance bands resolve to roles; bands include very_high, high, medium, low, very_low, zero, sink, and unknown; visualization can use the metric without changing solver semantics.
close_reason: Landed role metrics surface and loader exposure

title: Replace demand-only semantics with role activity drivers
description: Generalize role demand into activity drivers so service/product demand, baseline scale factors, linked parent activity, and exogenous series can all drive role activity.
acceptance_criteria: Role activity driver schema exists; residual methods can use baseline scale factors; linked child roles can inherit parent activity; existing demand-only assumptions are no longer hard-coded as the only activity source.
close_reason: Landed role activity driver surface and loader use

title: Add representation incumbent schema and base-year incumbent mix rules
description: Add representation incumbent metadata so a pathway representation can identify its incumbent method and a technology representation can identify its incumbent mix.
acceptance_criteria: A representation incumbent surface exists; incumbent rows reference methods in the same representation; incumbent shares sum to 1.0 per direct representation and anchor year; active roles force the anchor year to the incumbent method or mix.
close_reason: Landed representation incumbent surface and validation

title: Add physical system node, role membership, and physical edge schemas
description: Add canonical library schemas and CSV surfaces for physical system nodes, role memberships, and physical edges. These surfaces define navigation and physical-flow context without relying on reporting allocations.
acceptance_criteria: Schemas exist for physical system nodes, role memberships, and physical edges; validation enforces resolvable IDs, acyclic hierarchy nodes, and clear edge kinds; reporting allocations are not used to create hierarchy.
close_reason: Landed physical system graph schemas and CSV surfaces

title: Design physical doing-word role graph
description: Design the first physical role graph for the Australian energy and emissions system using verb/object phrases at every level. Treat current library elements as migration candidates rather than the design skeleton.
acceptance_criteria: A proposed role graph exists with doing-word names at every level; process heat is handled as host-role decomposition rather than a standalone national heat system unless a real shared heat system is modeled; export-gate resource supply is represented at the Australian export boundary.
close_reason: Landed physical doing-word role graph design

title: Define physical role graph library structure
description: Define the new physical role graph and representation semantics for the library. The structure should use doing-word role and group names, be based on physical energy and emissions flows rather than reporting sectors, and require no backward compatibility with old role or sector-shaped surfaces.
acceptance_criteria: Roles and groups use doing-word names; the physical hierarchy is not derived from reporting sectors; active direct representations use zero/one/many method rules; 2025 active roles use incumbent method or incumbent mix; residuals are methods, not a representation kind; role metrics expose computed emissions importance bands; no family/state compatibility layer remains.
close_reason: All physical role graph structure child issues are complete

title: Restore WebUI after role-first refactor
description: The role-first WebUI refactor left repo-backed user configs with stale autonomous efficiency role IDs and broke the Explorer system-flow graph layout fallback, causing failed predefined user config solves and pixelated stacked graph rendering. Implement migration, config updates, layout fallback, and regression tests.
close_reason: Implemented runtime migration for legacy autonomous role IDs and stale role-control drafts, fixed system-flow graph fallback/container sizing, added CLI and layout regressions, and verified all listed configs solve.

title: Role-first Library WebUI cutover
description: Refactor the WebUI to make roles, representations, and methods the user-facing structure, remove legacy sector/state compatibility projections, update solver/result terminology, add the role graph, and refresh tests/exports/docs for the role-first model.

title: Fix WebUI missing family validation summary asset
description: The Vite dev server crashes because the WebUI attempts to read sector_trajectory_library/validation/family_validation_summary.csv, but that file may be absent in a normal checkout. Investigate the import path and make the WebUI load reliably.
close_reason: Added an ESRL cutover regression test that fails active WebUI sources or the canonical manifest if retired sector_trajectory_library/family_validation_summary assets reappear; verified clean dev load, lint, build, and full web tests.

title: Document ESRL role-topology plan and link beads
description: Create a durable docs/plan reference for the ESRL role-topology library and demonstration optimizer plan, then link the ESRL epics and subissues to that document so implementers have full context.
acceptance_criteria: A docs/plan document captures the agreed ontology, epics, issue breakdown, dependencies, and implementation expectations; all ESRL beads reference the plan via spec-id and notes; changes are committed and pushed.
notes: Full implementation context and canonical semantics are in docs/plan/20260428-esrl-role-topology-plan.md. Use that plan as the source of truth for ontology, dependencies, no-backward-compatibility scope, and acceptance expectations.
close_reason: Created docs/plan/20260428-esrl-role-topology-plan.md and linked all ESRL epics/subissues to it with spec-id and notes.

title: ESRL 2.6: End-to-end validation and terminology cutover
description: Parent epic: simple-msm-esrl-2. Complete the role-topology cutover across library, optimizer, UI, tests, and documentation with no backward-compatibility layer.
design: Run the full test suite and targeted terminology searches. Ensure canonical code, UI labels, schema, and docs use role/method/representation terms. Compare crude-steel pathway-bundle and decomposed representation results as an acceptance demonstration.
acceptance_criteria: Full test suite passes; canonical code/UI/docs no longer use state or family for model semantics; crude-steel pathway and decomposed representation results can be compared; no backward-compatibility loader or aliases remain.
notes: Full implementation context and canonical semantics are in docs/plan/20260428-esrl-role-topology-plan.md. Use that plan as the source of truth for ontology, dependencies, no-backward-compatibility scope, and acceptance expectations.
close_reason: Implemented end-to-end ESRL cutover validation: canonical config/app-config terminology scans, role-growth preset cleanup, crude-steel decomposition built-in comparison, and UI/docs copy updates. Verification: bun run test passed 380/380; bun run build passed; baseline and crude-steel decomposition CLI runs solved; compare command reported comparable aggregate/decomposed results; git diff --check and targeted canonical terminology scans passed. bun run lint remains blocked only by existing SystemFlowGraphCanvas React hook failures tracked in simple-msm-mjlo.

title: ESRL 2.5: Convert built-in configurations to role controls
description: Parent epic: simple-msm-esrl-2. Convert built-in web configurations to canonical role controls and remove old service/state control fields.
design: Built-ins should use role_controls, active_method_ids, and representation_by_role. Remove service_controls and active_state_ids. Reference baseline must solve after conversion.
acceptance_criteria: Built-ins use role_controls, active_method_ids, and representation_by_role; old service_controls and active_state_ids are removed; reference baseline solves; built-in configuration tests cover the converted schema.
notes: Full implementation context and canonical semantics are in docs/plan/20260428-esrl-role-topology-plan.md. Use that plan as the source of truth for ontology, dependencies, no-backward-compatibility scope, and acceptance expectations.
close_reason: Converted bundled web configurations to canonical role_controls, active_method_ids, and representation_by_role while preserving runtime solve compatibility.

title: ESRL 2.4: Update role representation UI
description: Parent epic: simple-msm-esrl-2. Update the web UI so the demonstration app exposes role representations and methods instead of state/family controls.
design: Right sidebar should become role representation UI. It should show selected representation per role, show method chips for direct bundle representations, show child roles only when decomposition is active, and avoid user-facing state terminology.
acceptance_criteria: Right sidebar uses role/method/representation language; users can choose pathway bundle versus decomposition for crude steel; UI shows child roles only when decomposition is active; no user-facing state terminology remains.
notes: Full implementation context and canonical semantics are in docs/plan/20260428-esrl-role-topology-plan.md. Use that plan as the source of truth for ontology, dependencies, no-backward-compatibility scope, and acceptance expectations.
close_reason: Updated the workspace right sidebar to expose role representations, method chips, and decomposed child roles.

title: ESRL 2.3: Generalize optimizer balances for decomposed roles
description: Parent epic: simple-msm-esrl-2. Update the demonstration optimizer so decomposed roles behave like process chains with intermediate role outputs and demands.
design: Solver must handle role outputs that become inputs to other active roles. It must balance intermediate outputs such as direct reduced iron, prevent parent direct methods and decomposed child roles from both supplying the same role, and preserve existing required service and supply commodity behavior under the role ontology.
acceptance_criteria: Solver handles intermediate role outputs as commodities/services; decomposed crude-steel DRI supply and demand balance correctly; direct pathway methods and decomposed child roles cannot both supply the same parent role; infeasibility diagnostics identify missing child-role coverage.
notes: Full implementation context and canonical semantics are in docs/plan/20260428-esrl-role-topology-plan.md. Use that plan as the source of truth for ontology, dependencies, no-backward-compatibility scope, and acceptance expectations.
close_reason: Implemented generalized decomposed-role optimizer balances and diagnostics.

title: ESRL 2.2: Resolve active role structure from representation selections
description: Parent epic: simple-msm-esrl-2. Add a model-structure resolver that turns selected role representations into active roles and active methods.
design: Configuration stores representation_by_role. The resolver activates direct methods for pathway_bundle and technology_bundle representations, or child roles for role_decomposition representations. It must reject double coverage, inactive required roles, and missing representation selections.
acceptance_criteria: Configuration supports representation_by_role; resolver activates direct methods or child roles according to selected representation; resolver rejects double coverage and missing role coverage; tests cover crude-steel pathway versus decomposition selection.
notes: Full implementation context and canonical semantics are in docs/plan/20260428-esrl-role-topology-plan.md. Use that plan as the source of truth for ontology, dependencies, no-backward-compatibility scope, and acceptance expectations.
close_reason: Implemented representation_by_role role-topology resolver and wired solve/status filtering through active role structure.

title: ESRL 2.1: Load canonical role-topology library in the web app
description: Parent epic: simple-msm-esrl-2. Replace the web package loader with canonical role-topology loading. Do not keep the old family/state loader path.
design: Read roles, representations, role_decomposition_edges, reporting_allocations, methods, method_years, and role demand files. TypeScript model should use role/method naming throughout the app data layer.
acceptance_criteria: Web loader reads canonical role-topology files; TypeScript model uses role/method naming; old family/state loader path is removed; loader tests cover roles, representations, methods, and method-year parsing.
notes: Full implementation context and canonical semantics are in docs/plan/20260428-esrl-role-topology-plan.md. Use that plan as the source of truth for ontology, dependencies, no-backward-compatibility scope, and acceptance expectations.

acceptance_criteria: README explains the public ontology; schema docs match canonical files; validation checks topology, representation exclusivity, method-year completeness, and reporting allocation resolution; search confirms canonical library docs do not use family/state as model semantics.
notes: Full implementation context and canonical semantics are in docs/plan/20260428-esrl-role-topology-plan.md. Use that plan as the source of truth for ontology, dependencies, no-backward-compatibility scope, and acceptance expectations.

title: ESRL 1.5: Author crude-steel role-decomposition pilot

design: produce_crude_steel keeps a pathway_bundle representation for current aggregate methods. Add a decomposition representation that activates child roles for non-H2 crude-steel aggregate coverage, produce_direct_reduced_iron, and melt_refine_dri_crude_steel. Child roles must be represented by pathway or technology bundles with explicit method-year data.
acceptance_criteria: produce_crude_steel has pathway-bundle and role-decomposition representations; decomposition activates DRI and melt/refine child roles; non-H2 crude-steel coverage remains explicit; method-year data preserves complete crude-steel coverage.
notes:

title: 2025 incumbent forcing activates demand for disabled subsectors

This wakes up outputs that the config intentionally disabled (e.g. cement in agriculture-only). Since demand resolution builds tables for all demand-required outputs, those newly-activated outputs get positive demand causing infeasible solves. Fix: gate the 2025 incumbent forcing so it only applies to outputs that have configured active states in at least one year.

title: Unify solver scoping: remove seed/scoped/full-model distinction
description:
## Problem

The solver has a 'scoped run' vs 'full model run' dichotomy in buildSolveRequest.ts and solveScope.ts. When any required_service output has 0 active states (e.g. removals disabled with active_state_ids=[]), deriveSeedOutputIds() returns a seed array triggering 'scoped mode'. The scoped path in filterSolveRequestForOutputs() drops external commodity demands for auto-included dependencies like electricity, because it filters externalCommodityDemandByCommodity by activeRequiredServiceIds (seeds only) rather than all in-run outputs.

This causes the-base-case config to lose electricity external demand (~144M MWh) when removals are disabled, producing different results from the-full-monty even though electricity is actively in the solve in both.

## Design

Replace the seed/scoped/full-model branching with a single unified inclusion algorithm:

1. Collect all outputs with >=1 active state → directly included
2. Auto-include supply commodities (electricity etc.) when any in-run row needs them as an input
3. Filter ALL config data (rows, demands, external commodity demands, controls) by the final included set — uniformly, no special cases

No output is special. Demand is optional (removals have none). Pinned (1 state) is just N=1. Externalized/optimize is a per-commodity choice, not a role distinction.

## Files to change

- web/src/solver/solveScope.ts — delete deriveSeedOutputIds(), add deriveIncludedOutputIds(), simplify OutputRunParticipation types
- web/src/solver/buildSolveRequest.ts — remove seed/full-model branch, single path through filterSolveRequestForOutputs filtering by includedOutputIds
- web/src/solver/solveRequestModel.ts — decouple serviceDemandByOutput from demand_required flag
- web/src/components/workspace/rightSidebarStatus.ts — replace full_model/seed_scope with active_pathways
- web/test/solveSubsets.test.mjs — add regression test, update helpers

## Acceptance criteria

- Disabling removals (active_state_ids=[]) does not drop electricity external demand
- the-base-case and the-full-monty CLI results show correct electricity external demand when electricity mode=optimize
- No seed/scoped/full-model distinction in code
- All existing tests pass
- New regression test confirms the fix
close_reason: Unified solver scoping. Removed seed/scoped/full-model distinction. External commodity demands now preserved for all in-run outputs. All 66 tests pass.

title: 4/7 — CLI: use runScenario() and include overlay contributions
description:
The CLI (solve.mjs) currently:
- Loads configs with its own validateConfig() instead of parseConfigurationDocument
- Never calls ensureResidualOverlays
- Never loads residual_overlays_2025.csv
- Never builds overlay contribution rows
- Reports solver-only emissions (no overlays)

This means CLI and web UI give different answers for the same config.

Fix: Refactor solve.mjs to call the shared runScenario() helper. This requires:
1. Loading the overlay CSV in loadPackage() (add it to the pkg object)
2. Calling runScenario() instead of the manual buildSolveRequest+solveWithLpAdapter chain
3. Reporting emissions from the ExecutionSnapshot contributions (which include overlays)
4. Add a --solver-only flag for users who want raw LP output without overlays

Depends on: runScenario() helper existing.

title: 3/7 — Web UI: use solved config snapshot instead of live currentConfiguration
description:
THE BUG FIX. In ConfigurationWorkspaceCenter.tsx, buildAllContributionRows is called with the live currentConfiguration from the store. This means when you switch configs, the overlay computation runs against the NEW config but the OLD solve result, causing mismatched emissions until the re-solve completes.

Fix: Store the ConfigurationDocument snapshot alongside the SolveRequest/SolveResult in the solve hook (useConfigurationSolve.ts). Use that snapshot — not currentConfiguration — when computing contribution rows.

Approach options (pick one):
A. Have useConfigurationSolve store the full ExecutionSnapshot (preferred — uses the new runScenario helper)
B. Have useConfigurationSolve store a solvedConfiguration alongside request/result, pass that to buildAllContributionRows

Either way, ConfigurationWorkspaceCenter.tsx must stop reading currentConfiguration for post-solve computation.

Depends on: runScenario() helper existing.

title: 2/7 — Create unified runScenario() helper
description:
Create a single runScenario() function that is the canonical way to execute a scenario from config to results. Both CLI and web UI will call this.

Steps:
1. Normalize/resolve the configuration document (resolveConfigurationDocument)
2. Ensure residual overlays are populated (ensureResidualOverlays)
3. Build the solve request (buildSolveRequest)
4. Solve the LP (solveWithLpAdapter)
5. Build all contribution rows including overlays (buildAllContributionRows)
6. Return an ExecutionSnapshot

Signature sketch:
```ts
export function runScenario(
  pkg: PackageData,
  configuration: ConfigurationDocument,
  options?: { includeOverlays?: boolean },
): ExecutionSnapshot;
```

Place in web/src/solver/ or web/src/results/. This must work in both browser (main thread or worker) and Node.js (CLI) contexts.

Depends on: ExecutionSnapshot type existing.

title: 1/7 — Introduce ExecutionSnapshot type
Epic: Unify scenario execution — fix 2025 emissions mismatch between base case and full Monty
description: Base-year 2025 emissions differ between the reference (base case) and full-model-incumbents (full Monty) configurations, especially in the power sector. Root cause: the web UI uses a live mutable currentConfiguration for post-solve overlay computation instead of the snapshot that produced the solve result, and the CLI omits overlays entirely. This epic introduces an ExecutionSnapshot type, unifies CLI/web into a single runScenario() code path, eliminates stale-config provenance bugs, and removes dead seed_output_ids metadata.

title: Fix run-page crash from persistent y-axis snapshot loop
description: The new Run-page y-axis persistence hook uses useSyncExternalStore with a snapshot that returns a freshly parsed array on each read. In React 19 this triggers 'The result of getSnapshot should be cached to avoid an infinite loop', causing the Run page to show a loading state and then crash to a blank screen. Stabilize the snapshot and verify the Run page renders normally.
acceptance_criteria: Run page no longer crashes after loading charts; browser console no longer reports getSnapshot infinite loop; targeted chart tests, lint, and build pass.

title: [2/4-C] Integrate package-derived anchors into loadPackage and loadAppConfig

Update the app loading flow so that package-derived anchors take precedence over the hardcoded JSON file, while preserving the JSON as a fallback.

In web/src/data/packageLoader.ts loadPackage():

2. Merge the result INTO appConfig.baseline_activity_anchors:
   - Package-derived anchors override JSON values for the same key
   - JSON-only entries (like land_sequestration, engineered_removals) are preserved
3. This merged appConfig is what gets returned in PackageData

The merge should be done BEFORE defaultConfiguration is loaded (since loadDefaultConfiguration may reference anchors).

Also update web/solve.mjs:
- The CLI solver loads baseline_activity_anchors.json directly. It should also parse the CSV and merge, OR restructure so it goes through the same loadPackage() path.
- Check how solve.mjs currently loads the anchors and make it consistent with the browser path.

title: [2/4-B] Create packageAnchorMapping.ts to derive baseline anchors from CSV
description:
Create a new utility module web/src/data/packageAnchorMapping.ts that maps ServiceDemandAnchorRow[] to the existing BaselineActivityAnchor format.

The mapping logic should be:
1. For rows with anchor_type === 'service_demand_anchor':
   - Map to anchor_kind: 'service_demand'
   - Key by service_or_output_name
   - output_role: look up from output_roles config, or default to 'required_service'
   - anchor_year: 2025
   - value: quantity_2025
   - unit: from the CSV row
   - provenance_note: combine source_family + coverage_note

2. For the row with anchor_type === 'external_electricity_balance_anchor':
   - Map to key 'electricity', anchor_kind: 'external_commodity_demand'
   - output_role: 'endogenous_supply_commodity'
   - value: quantity_2025 (this is 144018720 MWh)

3. Skip rows with anchor_type === 'derived_explicit_electricity_load' and 'electricity_output_benchmark' — these are informational only.

Export a function:
```ts
export function deriveBaselineAnchorsFromPackage(
  rows: ServiceDemandAnchorRow[]
): Record<string, BaselineActivityAnchor>
```

The function should also handle optional_activity outputs (land_sequestration, engineered_removals) — these won't be in the CSV, so the caller should merge with the existing JSON fallbacks.

Depends on: 2A

title: [2/4-A] Add ServiceDemandAnchorRow type and parse service_demand_anchors_2025.csv
[Epic 2/4] Add explicit present-day demand-side anchor table from package
description:
Incorporate service_demand_anchors_2025.csv as the authoritative source for 2025 demand/activity anchors, replacing the hardcoded values in web/src/app_config/baseline_activity_anchors.json. The CSV provides anchors with provenance, implied energy/emissions, and default state mappings.

title: Remove fixed_shares control mode entirely
description: Tear out fixed_shares as a ConfigurationControlMode. Migrate all builtin configs from fixed_shares to optimize+active_state_ids. Remove from types, pathway semantics, store, solver contract, LP adapter, UI, schemas, and app config.

title: Migrate built-in configs and schema: disabled_state_ids → active_state_ids

title: Simplify left sidebar: remove disabled rows, update fixed-share panel
description: In LeftSidebar.tsx: (1) Remove enabledStateIds, capDenominatorStateIds, showsSolveActivitySplit, (2) Remove 'disabled' rows/badges in fixed-share panel, (3) Show all states with share inputs always editable, (4) Per row: share > 0 = active, share = 0 = inactive, (5) Update copy: '0% share means inactive', 'Cap normalization uses active pathways only'.

title: Simplify right sidebar UI: show only Active/Inactive, remove enabled badges
description: In rightSidebarStatus.ts, rightSidebarTree.ts, RightSidebarContent.tsx: (1) Remove enabled-pathway badge and solve-active split badge, (2) Remove all 'enabled but not solve-active' copy and 'off' references, (3) Show only 'N active pathways' or 'No active pathways', (4) Chips become Active/Inactive only, (5) Remove enabledStateIds, capDenominatorStateIds, showsSolveActivitySplit, (6) In fixed_shares mode sidebar is display-only (share inputs are the editor), (7) Rename onToggleStateEnabled → onToggleStateActive.

title: Update solver internals: lpAdapter, solveRequestModel, fixedShareValidation
description: (1) lpAdapter.ts: replace all disabled/available/capEligible usage with activeStateIds. buildEffectiveMaxShareLookup uses active states as denominator. Inactive rows get zero bounds. Rename diagnostics text ('disabled state' → 'inactive state'). (2) solveRequestModel.ts: resolveControlForYear resolves activeStateIds from active_state_ids, remove disabledStateIds. (3) fixedShareValidation.ts: remove disabled-state checks, keep only state-exists / share-non-negative / total-equals-1 / at-least-one-positive-share. (4) contract.ts: ResolvedSolveControl.disabledStateIds → activeStateIds.

title: Update solveScope.ts: remove available/capEligible fields, simplify demand participation
description: In web/src/solver/solveScope.ts: (1) Remove availableStateIds, availableStateCount, capEligibleStateIds, capEligibleStateCount from DerivedOutputRunStatus, (2) Keep only activeStateIds and activeStateCount, (3) Rename 'no_enabled_pathways' → 'no_active_pathways' in OutputDemandParticipation, (4) Derive sector/subsector enabled/disabled purely from activeStateCount > 0.

title: Update packageStore: toggleStateEnabled → toggleStateActive, remove disabled logic
description: In web/src/data/packageStore.ts: (1) Rename toggleStateEnabled to toggleStateActive, (2) Replace disabled_state_ids logic with active_state_ids logic, (3) Remove getAvailableStateIds / reconcileControlForAvailableStates, (4) For fixed_shares mode, activity is driven by share inputs only (no separate toggle), (5) When switching to fixed_shares, seed equal shares from current active states, (6) When switching away, capture positive-share states into active_state_ids, (7) Update configurationsEqual() to sort active_state_ids.

title: Simplify pathwaySemantics.ts: remove available/capEligible, keep only activeStateIds
description: Rewrite derivePathwayStateIds() to return only { allStateIds, activeStateIds }. Remove availableStateIds and capEligibleStateIds. Active logic: optimize → all states with active_state_ids filter; fixed_shares → states with share > 0; externalized → empty. Remove 'off' case. Update PathwayControlInput to use activeStateIds instead of disabledStateIds.

title: Update types: replace disabled_state_ids with active_state_ids, remove 'off' mode
description: In web/src/data/types.ts: (1) Remove 'off' from ConfigurationControlMode, (2) Remove disabled_state_ids from ConfigurationServiceControl and ConfigurationServiceControlYearOverride, (3) Add active_state_ids?: string[] | null to both. Semantics: null/undefined = all states active, [] = none active. This is the foundational type change that all other tasks depend on.

title: Epic: Simplify pathway state model to active/inactive only
description: Remove the enabled/disabled/available layer from the pathway state model. Currently we have 3 layers (all / enabled-available / active) plus a separate cap-eligible concept. This is over-engineered for the use case. Simplify to just 2 layers: all-states and active-states. Key changes: (1) Replace disabled_state_ids with active_state_ids in ConfigurationServiceControl, (2) Remove 'off' mode, (3) In fixed_shares mode share>0 = active and share=0 = inactive, (4) Cap normalization denominator = active states only, (5) UI shows only Active/Inactive — no separate 'enabled' concept, (6) Sector/subsector enabled/disabled is purely derived from activeStateCount > 0.

title: Investigate inconsistent pathway semantics across solver and UI
description: The UI and solver disagree on pathway availability/active/selected/cap semantics, e.g. agriculture livestock output bundle shows two visible/selected pathways while the panel reports one active pathway and the cap chart behaves like only one pathway is in the denominator. Investigate the discrepancy across pathway semantics, solve scope, reporting, and UI rendering; align definitions and implement a consistent model if needed.

title: Fix fixed-share max-share denominator semantics
description: Exact-share controls currently keep zero-share available states in the max-share normalization denominator, which makes many built-in scenarios infeasible even though they pin a single active state. Update pathway semantics so fixed-share controls normalize max-share caps over active positive-share states, then adjust regression coverage and verify built-in CLI cases.

title: Migrate public asset filenames and persisted draft keys away from scenario naming
description:
## Why
Oracle identified stale `scenario` naming in public/static asset filenames and persistence keys, but these areas may have compatibility implications and should be handled as explicit migration work rather than incidental rename work.

## What
Apply the compatibility policy to rename or alias remaining public/persisted `scenario` surfaces safely.

Representative targets include:
- `web/src/app_config/reference_scenario.json`
- `web/src/app_config/scenario_schema.json`
- `web/public/app_config/reference_scenario.json`
- `web/public/app_config/scenario_schema.json`
- versioned public variants such as `reference_scenario_v02.json` and `scenario_schema_v02.json`
- `web/src/data/scenarioDraftStorage.ts`

This issue should also address compatibility handling for persisted keys such as `simple-msm.scenario-draft.v2` and legacy payload fields such as `baseConfigurationScenario`.
acceptance_criteria:
- Public asset filenames and persisted draft/storage surfaces are migrated or intentionally aliased per the compatibility policy
- Existing users and any asset consumers retain a safe compatibility path where needed

title: Rename solver and compare contracts from scenario to configuration
description:
## Why
Oracle found `scenario` naming embedded in solver contracts, compare flows, worker payloads, and internal result/report terminology. These are more coupled than simple UI renames and should move only after the shared model terminology is stable.

## What
Rename solver/compare contract terminology from `scenario` to `configuration` where compatibility allows.

Representative targets include:
- `web/src/solver/contract.ts`
- `web/src/solver/buildSolveRequest.ts`
- `web/src/solver/solveRequestModel.ts`
- `web/src/solver/solveScope.ts`
- `web/src/solver/lpAdapter.ts`
- `web/src/solver/solver.worker.ts`
- `web/src/results/chartData.ts`
- `web/src/compare/compareAnalysis.ts`
- compare-related page code

Representative symbols include request payload fields like `request.scenario`, `ResolvedScenarioForSolve`, compare-side `Scenario*` types, and raw artifact labels such as `scenario_lp` where they are not protected by compatibility requirements.
acceptance_criteria:
- Solver and compare contracts are configuration-first after the shared data-model rename lands
- Any compatibility-sensitive serialized artifact names are handled according to the policy issue

title: Rename shared data-model terminology from scenario to configuration
description:
## Why
Oracle identified the shared data/model layer as the main source of mixed terminology. UI code already talks about configurations in many places, but the underlying types, loaders, and storage helpers still use `Scenario*` naming heavily.

## What
Rename foundational shared model concepts so the codebase stops mixing `configuration` in the UI with `scenario` in core data structures.

Representative targets from the audit include:
- `web/src/data/types.ts`
- `web/src/data/demandResolution.ts`
- `web/src/data/scenarioLoader.ts`
- `web/src/data/scenarioWorkspaceModel.ts`
- `web/src/data/scenarioDraftStorage.ts`
- `web/src/data/packageStore.ts`
- `web/src/data/packageLoader.ts`
- adjacent configuration loaders/metadata modules where names are mixed

Representative symbols include:
- `ScenarioDocument`
- `ScenarioControlMode`
- `SCENARIO_YEARS`
- `parseScenarioDocument`
- `validateScenarioDocument`
- `defaultScenario`
- `createConfigurationFromScenario`
- scenario draft helper names and result shapes
acceptance_criteria:
- Shared types and helpers use `configuration` terminology consistently where compatibility does not require otherwise
- Foundational model renames are settled before dependent UI and solver rename work proceeds

title: Define compatibility policy for remaining scenario-named assets and persistence
description:
## Why
Some remaining `scenario` references are not simple search-and-replace candidates. Oracle flagged public asset filenames, persisted browser draft keys, legacy migration fields, and possibly solver artifact names as compatibility-sensitive.

## What
Document the terminology boundary and decide what must remain temporarily compatible versus what can be renamed outright.

This issue should cover decisions for at least:
- public `app_config/*scenario*` filenames
- persisted browser draft keys
- legacy persisted fields such as `baseConfigurationScenario`
- raw solver artifact naming like `scenario_lp`, if anything external depends on it
- whether archival docs under `docs/prd/**` are exempt from terminology cleanup
acceptance_criteria:
- A clear compatibility policy is recorded for the remaining `scenario`-named assets and persistence surfaces
- The policy distinguishes temporary compatibility shims from direct rename targets
- Downstream cleanup issues can proceed without re-litigating the compatibility boundary

title: LeftSidebar: expose endogenous supply mode and disable commodity price selectors when in-model
description:
Why this issue exists
The workspace exposes commodity price selectors even when an endogenous supply commodity such as electricity is configured to stay in-model. In those cases the selected exogenous commodity price is not the operative supply treatment for that commodity, so leaving the selector active is misleading and obscures whether the commodity is externalized, fixed-shares, or optimize in the current configuration.

What needs to be done
Expose the effective mode for endogenous supply commodities in the workspace UI for the current configuration. In the left sidebar, show whether each endogenous supply commodity is externalized or in-model, and if it is in-model (for example fixed_shares or optimize), render its commodity price selector as non-selectable and visually deselected so the UI does not imply that an exogenous price is currently driving the solve. Keep the selector available only when the commodity is externalized.
acceptance_criteria:
- Derived status for endogenous supply commodities is visible to the workspace UI for the active configuration
- The left sidebar clearly shows the current mode for endogenous supply commodities such as electricity
- When an endogenous supply commodity is in-model, its commodity price selector is non-selectable and shows no active price selection
- When an endogenous supply commodity is externalized, its commodity price selector remains selectable and reflects the active scenario price level
- Buildings-only endogenous electricity no longer implies that the electricity price selector is active for the current solve treatment

title: Show demand participation separately and validate active demand with no enabled pathways
description:
Why this issue exists
The current workspace does not clearly separate pathway availability from whether demand is active in the current run. For required-service outputs this leaves no obvious explanation for states that remain configured while the demand is excluded by scope, and it also leaves room for positive demand with no enabled pathways to be presented ambiguously.

What needs to be done
Expose demand participation status in the workspace separately from state enablement, and add validation for required-service outputs that are active in the current run but have no enabled pathways. Commodity outputs should also communicate endogenous versus externalized supply without reading as disabled.
acceptance_criteria:
- Required-service outputs expose demand status such as active in this run, excluded from this run, or no enabled pathways
- The UI warns or blocks solve when a required-service output has positive in-run demand and zero enabled states
- Excluded outputs do not appear to have active demand in the current run
- Commodity outputs communicate endogenous versus externalized supply separately from disabled status
- Regression coverage includes a case with positive required-service demand and zero enabled states

title: RightSidebar: distinguish excluded-from-run, dependency, and disabled outputs
description:
Why this issue exists
The State Selector currently dims outputs that are merely outside the seed scope using the same visual treatment it uses for truly disabled outputs. That makes auto-included electricity look disabled in buildings-only endogenous runs and makes excluded sectors such as passenger road transport look like disabled-but-enabled hybrids.

What needs to be done
Update the RightSidebar to consume the shared derived output/run status and render scope participation separately from state enablement. The sidebar should only mark an output as disabled when it has zero enabled states, and it should explicitly communicate excluded outputs and auto-included dependencies.
acceptance_criteria:
- Out-of-scope outputs no longer share the same badge/dimming treatment as truly disabled outputs
- An output gets a disabled badge only when it has zero enabled states
- State chip on/off styling is driven by enabled state status rather than raw scope membership
- The sidebar clearly differentiates in-scope outputs, auto-included dependencies, excluded outputs, and disabled outputs
- In the buildings-only endogenous configuration, electricity is shown as an auto-included dependency and passenger road transport is shown as excluded from the run rather than disabled
- The sidebar includes short explanatory copy or legend text for the new statuses

title: Add shared derived output/run status for scoped solves
description:
Why this issue exists
The current workspace derives output status ad hoc in the sidebar, while the solver separately expands scoped outputs to include endogenous dependencies such as electricity. That split is the root cause of the current UI confusion.

What needs to be done
Create a shared helper that derives per output: enabled state ids/count, whether the output is truly disabled, whether it is a seed-scoped output, an auto-included dependency, excluded from the current run, or part of a full-model run, plus its demand participation status and control mode. The helper should reuse the same dependency-expansion semantics as the solve request builder so the UI and solver stay aligned.
acceptance_criteria:
- A shared helper derives output/run status from the current configuration and package data
- Derived status distinguishes seed scope, auto-included dependency, excluded from run, and full-model participation
- Derived status also exposes enabled state ids/count, disabled output status, control mode, and demand participation
- The buildings-only endogenous configuration derives residential/commercial as seed-scoped, electricity as an auto-included dependency, and passenger road transport as excluded from run
- The implementation reuses the same dependency-expansion logic used for scoped solve requests

title: Clarify state selector scope, enablement, and demand semantics
description:
Problem
The workspace currently conflates scoped-solve selection, true state disablement, endogenous dependency expansion, and demand participation. In practice this makes outputs like electricity in a buildings-only endogenous solve appear disabled-ish even though they are auto-included and active, while excluded sectors like passenger road transport still show enabled states and read as quasi-disabled.

Outcome
Establish one clear semantics model for scope, effective run inclusion, true disablement, and demand participation, then reflect that consistently in the workspace UI and tests.
acceptance_criteria:
- A single implementation epic tracks the scope/enablement/demand clarification work
- Child issues cover derived run status, sidebar rendering, demand status/validation, and metadata/docs/tests
- Child issue dependencies reflect the intended implementation order

title: Default reference scenario is infeasible on Results page
description:
## Why this issue exists
Using the web app with the packaged default scenario immediately lands the Results page in an infeasible solver state. A first-run user cannot get a successful baseline solve from the shipped scenario, which breaks the core explorer workflow.

## Reproduction
1. Start the Vite app from `web/` and open the app.
2. Leave the packaged reference scenario unchanged.
3. Click `Results`.
4. Observe the page render `SCENARIO LP error` with `service_and_supply_lp_not_optimal`.

## Observed behavior
The diagnostics report repeated `exact_control_share_conflict` and `supply_fixed_share_conflict` errors where fixed/pinned shares in the reference scenario exceed the library row `max_share` caps across 2030-2050. Examples visible in the UI include residential/commercial buildings, passenger/freight road, industrial heat, cement, steel, agriculture, and electricity supply.

## Expected behavior
The packaged reference scenario should solve successfully on the Results page, or the app should prevent and flag incompatible controls before attempting an impossible solve.

## Acceptance criteria
- Visiting `Results` with the shipped reference scenario no longer fails immediately with infeasible fixed-share conflicts.
- The user gets either a successful baseline solve or a clear pre-solve validation state that identifies and blocks incompatible controls before the worker run.
- Any tests added cover the regression at the highest useful layer.
notes: Shipped the default reference scenario as a stable baseline by externalizing electricity and relaxing max-share enforcement for the incumbent-locked baseline. Added a regression test that runs the real packaged reference scenario through the solver path and asserts the Results baseline stays optimal (partial because optional removals remain outside the LP core). Follow-up tracked in simple-msm-vvt for the deeper endogenous-electricity infeasibility/diagnostics issue.
close_reason: Fixed on main in 162c9e1. The shipped reference scenario now uses externalized electricity with relaxed max-share enforcement so the default Results run reaches an optimal baseline again; browser verification passed on the worktree dev server. Follow-up: simple-msm-vvt.

title: 7/7 Migration and release notes for ordering change
description:
Document the behavioural change so the team understands the new ordering is expected, not a regression.

## What to do
- Add a note to internal changelog / release notes explaining:
  - Additionality ordering semantics changed from forward-greedy (base→target, largest delta first) to reverse-greedy (target→base, smallest delta removed first, then reversed for display)
  - The same configuration pair may now produce a different atom order
  - The new order reflects target-state importance: atoms that matter most in the final optimised state appear first
- Refresh any screenshots, docs, or analyst expectations tied to the old ordering

## What not to do
- No persisted UI state migration needed (no mode toggle being added)
- useAdditionalityAnalysis.ts, appUiStore.ts do not need changes
- No schema migration

## Acceptance criteria
- Team understands changed ordering is intentional
- No unnecessary state/version migration work introduced

title: 6/7 Add regression tests for reverse-greedy ordering
description:
Add targeted tests in web/test/additionalityAnalysis.test.mjs to prove the new reverse-greedy algorithm addresses the stated problem and handles edge cases.

## Test cases to add

### A. Core proof: reverse greedy fixes misleading ordering
Create a synthetic solver scoring case with two atoms where:
- From base: atom A has larger delta than atom B
- From target: removing B hurts more than removing A
Example objectives: base=0, A-only=10, B-only=6, A+B(target)=7
- Forward greedy would rank A first (delta 10 vs 6)
- Reverse greedy should rank B first (removing B from target: |7-0|=7 vs removing A: |7-6|=1; A is least important, removed first in reverse, placed last in presentation)
Assert that the final presented sequence has B before A.

### B. Full run reaches target metrics
Adapt existing 'greedy analysis reaches the target objective' test:
- Assert sequence[0].metricsBefore equals report.baseMetrics
- Assert sequence.at(-1).metricsAfter equals report.targetMetrics
- All deltas internally consistent (assertConsistentDelta)

### C. Disable-action atom coverage
Test where base has a state active and target disables it:
- Reverse execution must enable it during candidate testing
- Final presented sequence still shows the original atom action (disable)

### D. Skipped-candidate behaviour under reverse execution
Reuse existing skip pattern but verify:
- Analysis succeeds if enough reverse candidates remain solvable
- Displayed sequence remains internally consistent after reversal
- Skipped counts are preserved

### E. Deterministic tie-breaking
Two candidates with identical absObjectiveDelta: ordering must be stable via compareAtoms alphabetical fallback.

## Acceptance criteria
- Tests explicitly prove the new ranking semantics (test A is the critical one)
- Tests cover both enable and disable atoms
- Tests cover partial/skipped behaviour in reverse mode
- All existing passing tests still pass (may need adaptation for new ordering)

title: 5/7 Guard chart/model rendering on sequenceComplete
description:
Ensure the waterfall chart builders and table renderers only operate on complete sequences.

## Files
- web/src/pages/AdditionalityPage.tsx — add guard around waterfall/table rendering: only render when report.sequenceComplete === true
- web/src/pages/additionalityPageModel.ts — no changes expected if gated upstream, but verify buildAdditionalityWaterfallRows() and buildAdditionalityReferenceRows() still work unchanged for full successful reports

- No unnecessary refactor in additionalityPageModel.ts
- Waterfall charts and tables continue to work unchanged for full successful reports
- Incomplete reports do not attempt to render waterfall/table data

title: 4/7 Update additionality page copy for target-context semantics
description:
Update user-facing copy on the additionality page to accurately describe the new reverse-greedy ordering.

## File: web/src/pages/AdditionalityPage.tsx

Review and update any copy that describes the sequence as a forward greedy build-up. Current wording to look for includes references to 'greedy transition sequence' or 'builds the difference'.

## Suggested direction
- Explain that the order is determined by removing atoms from the target configuration, then shown as a forward base→target sequence.
- Keep the 'path-dependent' warning.
- Add one short line that this is an ordering heuristic, not a causal attribution.

Example wording:
- 'Atoms are ordered by reverse-greedy elimination from the target configuration: items whose removal changes the target least are placed later, and the resulting order is then shown as a forward base→target sequence.'
- 'These waterfalls remain sequence-based and path-dependent; they are not a unique attribution of total delta.'

## Acceptance criteria
- No user-facing copy implies the order was selected by forward base→target greedy
- The page explains target-context ordering in one concise place
- Path-dependent disclaimer is retained

title: 3/7 Handle partial reverse runs safely
description:
Ensure that partial reverse-greedy runs (where some candidates fail mid-sequence) are not rendered as full base→target waterfalls.

## Problem
With reverse greedy, if the analysis stops early after removing only some atoms, the completed portion represents a suffix near the target, not a full base→target sequence. Blindly reversing and rendering it as a normal waterfall would imply a sequence starting from base when it actually starts from an intermediate configuration.

## Changes

### web/src/additionality/additionalityAnalysis.ts
- When the reverse loop cannot complete all atoms (all remaining candidates fail):
  - Set phase: 'partial'
  - Set report.sequenceComplete = false
  - Do NOT attempt to reverse/transform the partial removal sequence into a presentation sequence
  - Set report.sequence = [] (empty) for partial runs, since a partial reverse sequence cannot be meaningfully displayed as base→target
  - Still populate: baseMetrics, targetMetrics, solveCount, skippedCandidates, error message

### web/src/pages/AdditionalityPage.tsx
- Gate waterfall/table rendering on report.sequenceComplete === true (or equivalently, sequence.length > 0)
- For partial reports, show a clear message: 'Analysis could not reconstruct a full base→target ordering because some intermediate solves failed.'

### web/src/pages/additionalityPageModel.ts
- No changes expected if rendering is gated upstream

## Acceptance criteria
- No partial reverse result is ever rendered as a full base→target waterfall
- Partial state still shows base/target summary metrics, solve count, and error info
- UI message is informative and not misleading

title: 2/7 Add ordering metadata to AdditionalityReport
1/7 Refactor core algorithm to reverse-greedy target-context ordering
description:
Modify runAdditionalityAnalysis() in web/src/additionality/additionalityAnalysis.ts to use reverse-greedy selection instead of forward-greedy.

## Current behaviour
- Starts from baseConfiguration, enables atoms one at a time, picks largest absObjectiveDelta, repeats.

## New behaviour
1. Still solve both base and target first (no change).
2. Set currentConfiguration = targetConfiguration, currentMetrics = targetEvaluation.
3. For each remaining atom, evaluate the inverse action (enable→disable, disable→enable) on currentConfiguration.
4. Pick the candidate with the smallest absObjectiveDelta (least important to the final state).
5. Apply that removal, update currentConfiguration, repeat until all atoms are processed.
6. Reverse the internal removal sequence and transform into a forward base→target presentation sequence:
   - atom = original atom (not inverted)
   - step = renumbered 1..n
   - metricsBefore = removal entry's metricsAfter
   - metricsAfter = removal entry's metricsBefore
   - metricsDeltaFromCurrent = metricsAfter - metricsBefore (forward direction)
   - absObjectiveDelta unchanged

## Implementation details
- Add helper: invertAdditionalityAtomAction(action: AdditionalityAtomAction): AdditionalityAtomAction

title: Epic: Reverse-greedy additionality ordering (target-context)
description: Replace the forward-greedy base→target additionality ordering algorithm with a reverse-greedy target→base approach. The current algorithm starts from the base case and greedily enables atoms by largest objective delta, which can produce misleading orderings when substitution effects are present (e.g., electrification showing up first even though it's a minor contributor in the final state). The new algorithm starts from the target, disables atoms one at a time picking the smallest delta (least important), repeats until back at base, then reverses the sequence for base→target presentation. This ensures ordering reflects true importance in the final optimized state.

title: [5-E] Add overlay growth projection and driver mapping
description:
Create the overlay growth trajectory system that projects 2025 overlay values forward using the active demand-growth preset.

## New module: web/src/data/overlayProjection.ts

### Static driver mapping
Export OVERLAY_GROWTH_PROXY: Record<string, string[]> mapping each overlay_id to its proxy demand-growth output IDs:
- residential_other → ["residential_building_services"]
- commercial_other → ["commercial_building_services"]
- transport_other → ["passenger_road_transport", "freight_road_transport"]
- manufacturing_other → ["low_temperature_heat", "medium_temperature_heat", "high_temperature_heat", "crude_steel", "cement_equivalent"]
- mining_other → [] (fallback to preset average)
- construction_other → [] (fallback to preset average)
- water_waste_other → [] (fallback to preset average)
- other_other → [] (fallback to preset average)
- residual_agriculture_other → ["livestock_output_bundle", "cropping_horticulture_output_bundle"]
- residual_fugitives → [] (fallback to preset average)
- residual_ippu_other → ["low_temperature_heat", "medium_temperature_heat", "high_temperature_heat", "crude_steel", "cement_equivalent"]
- residual_waste → [] (fallback to preset average)
- residual_lulucf_sink → [] (fallback to 0% — sinks should not grow by default)

### Functions

resolveOverlayGrowthRates(overlayIds: string[], resolvedServiceGrowthRates: Record<string, number>): Record<string, number>
- For each overlay_id: if proxy IDs exist, average their rates; else average all resolved rates; LULUCF defaults to 0%

ProjectedOverlayRow interface:
- overlayId, overlayLabel, overlayDomain, officialAccountingBucket, year, commodity (string | null)
- finalEnergyPj, nativeQuantity, directEnergyEmissionsMtco2e, otherEmissionsMtco2e, carbonBillableEmissionsMtco2e
- commodityCostAudm2024, fixedNonCommodityCostAudm2024, totalCostExCarbonAudm2024

projectOverlayRows(rows: ResidualOverlayRow[], years: number[], growthRates: Record<string, number>): ProjectedOverlayRow[]
- For each row × year: scale all numeric anchor fields by (1 + rate/100)^(year - 2025)
- One growth rate per overlay_id, applied uniformly to all its commodity rows

### Design notes
- All commodity rows within an overlay_id scale by the same factor (preserves 2025 fuel mix)
- Do NOT use generateValueTable from demandResolution — its rounding is designed for demand anchors, not for scaling multiple fields by a common factor
- Use a simple growth-factor approach: factor = (1 + rate/100) ** (year - 2025)

## Acceptance
- Projection produces correct row counts: inputRows.length × years.length
- 2025 values match source anchors exactly
- Growth compounds correctly for future years

Replace the old split overlay types (ResidualEnergyOverlayRow, ResidualNonEnergyEmissionsOverlayRow) with a single unified type matching the new residual_overlays_2025.csv.

## Changes

### types.ts
- Add ResidualOverlayDomain type: "energy_residual" | "nonenergy_residual" | "net_sink"
- Add ResidualOverlayRow interface with all 21 CSV columns
- Add ConfigurationResidualOverlayControl interface: { included?: boolean }
- Add ConfigurationResidualOverlays interface: { controls_by_overlay_id: Record<string, ConfigurationResidualOverlayControl> }
- Add residual_overlays?: ConfigurationResidualOverlays to ConfigurationDocument
- Update PackageData: replace residualEnergyOverlays2025 + residualNonEnergyEmissionsOverlays2025 with residualOverlays2025: ResidualOverlayRow[]
- Remove old ResidualEnergyOverlayRow and ResidualNonEnergyEmissionsOverlayRow types

### CSV columns to type mapping
overlay_id, overlay_label, overlay_domain (ResidualOverlayDomain), official_accounting_bucket, year (number), commodity (string | null, empty string becomes null), final_energy_pj_2025 (number | null), native_unit (string), native_quantity_2025 (number | null), direct_energy_emissions_mtco2e_2025 (number | null), other_emissions_mtco2e_2025 (number | null), carbon_billable_emissions_mtco2e_2025 (number | null), default_price_basis (string), default_price_per_native_unit_aud_2024 (number | null), default_commodity_cost_audm_2024 (number | null), default_fixed_noncommodity_cost_audm_2024 (number | null), default_total_cost_ex_carbon_audm_2024 (number | null), default_include (boolean), allocation_method (string), cost_basis_note (string), notes (string)

## Acceptance
- Old overlay types removed
- New types compile
- ConfigurationDocument has optional residual_overlays field

title: [Epic 5] Integrate unified residual overlays into results and configuration
description:
Integrate the new unified residual_overlays_2025.csv into the app. This replaces the old separate overlay tables with a single commodity-level, cost-bearing overlay layer that participates in all chart types (fuel/commodity, emissions, cost).

## Context
See docs/prd/20261314-high_level_update_note_v2.md for the data-pack update rationale.

The new CSV has 39 rows: 34 energy_residual rows (8 overlay IDs × multiple commodities) and 5 nonenergy_residual/net_sink rows. Energy rows carry commodity quantities, emissions, and cost fields. Non-energy rows carry other_emissions and carbon_billable_emissions.

## Scope
1. Replace old overlay types and loaders with unified ResidualOverlayRow
2. Add overlay settings to ConfigurationDocument (persisted toggles)
3. Build growth trajectory projection using demand-growth presets
4. Create a ResultContributionRow pipeline for post-solve overlay injection
5. Refactor chart builders to use the unified contribution pipeline
6. Add overlay toggles to the workspace left sidebar
7. Update diagnostics and tests

## Acceptance Criteria
- All 39 overlay CSV rows load and parse correctly
- Overlay toggles persist in saved configurations
- Overlays appear in emissions, fuel/commodity, and cost charts
- Forward trajectories use the active demand-growth preset
- Solver behavior is completely unchanged
- Old overlay types and loaders are removed
close_reason: All 10 subtasks complete: unified ResidualOverlayRow type, CSV parsing, diagnostics refactor, test updates, growth projection, store/config integration, ResultContributionRow pipeline, chart builder refactor, sidebar toggles, and integration tests

title: Persist run-page y-axis domains across chart updates
description: The Run page charts currently recompute their y-axis domain from the latest solve result on every update. This makes it hard to compare configurations when toggling options because the scale shifts between solves. Add per-chart remembered y-axis domains on the Run page, persist them in the browser, expose a per-chart reset control, and keep the behavior scoped to workspace charts only.
acceptance_criteria: Run-page charts remember per-chart y-axis domains across configuration toggles in this browser; domains only expand outward until reset; each workspace chart exposes a reset y-axis range control; Library and Compare charts remain unchanged; tests cover persistence helpers and render-time reset affordances.

title: [3/4-B] Parse overlay and diagnostic CSV files in packageLoader.ts
description:
Add parsing for the 4 new CSV files in web/src/data/packageLoader.ts.

1. Add mapping functions (following the existing toSectorState pattern):
   - toResidualEnergyOverlayRow(row) — parse all columns, use parseBool for default_include, parseNum for numeric fields
   - toResidualNonEnergyEmissionsOverlayRow(row) — same pattern
   - toCommodityBalance2025Row(row) — all numeric fields are nullable
   - toEmissionsBalance2025Row(row) — all numeric fields are nullable

2. In loadPackage(), parse each CSV:
   - parseCsv(requirePackageFile('data/residual_energy_overlay_sectors_2025.csv')).map(toResidualEnergyOverlayRow)
   - parseCsv(requirePackageFile('data/residual_nonenergy_emissions_overlays_2025.csv')).map(toResidualNonEnergyEmissionsOverlayRow)
   - parseCsv(requirePackageFile('data/commodity_balance_2025.csv')).map(toCommodityBalance2025Row)
   - parseCsv(requirePackageFile('data/emissions_balance_2025.csv')).map(toEmissionsBalance2025Row)

3. Add all 4 arrays to the returned PackageData object.

4. Note: The glob pattern in packageLoader.ts already covers '../../../aus_phase1_sector_state_library/data/*.csv' so the files are already available via requirePackageFile.

Depends on: 3A

title: [3/4-C] Create balanceDiagnostics.ts for overlay aggregation and cross-checking

     totalResidualEnergyPj: number,      // sum of default-included energy overlay PJ

     totalResidualNonEnergyEmissions: number, // sum of default-included non-energy MtCO2e (excl LULUCF)

CRITICAL GUARDRAIL: This module must NOT export anything that could be used in buildSolveRequest.ts. It is purely diagnostic/presentation. Add a JSDoc comment at the top of the file stating this.
